component_identification.content: Components include modular island boards, invader board, fear and blight systems, minor and major power decks, invader cards, fear cards, spirit panels, spirit-specific starting power cards, presence discs, Dahan pieces, and invader pieces for explorers, towns, and cities. Setup uses player-count island layout, spirit selection, fear deck prep, invader deck staging, and printed setup icons on boards for Dahan, invaders, and blight. Each spirit must have full presence and unique cards because missing pieces break growth tracks and legal targeting.

core_game_loop.content: Each round runs in fixed order: Spirit Phase, Fast Powers, Invader Phase, Slow Powers, then Time Passes. In Spirit Phase players choose growth, gain energy, and play cards. Fast powers resolve before invaders. Invaders then ravage, build, and explore according to shown cards. Slow powers resolve after invaders and usually set up next round. Fear can trigger fear cards and raise terror level, changing victory requirements.

detailed_rules.content: Growth drives the economy of card plays, energy, and presence range. Card effects resolve only if target and range are legal. Element icons on played cards unlock innate powers when thresholds are met. Ravage deals damage in matching lands, blight is added when land damage threshold is reached, and Dahan counterattack if they survive. Build adds towns or cities where invaders are present in matching lands. Explore adds explorers from legal source lands into shown terrain. Fear is generated mainly by destroying invaders and by card text. Terror levels reduce what must be removed for victory. Adversaries and scenarios override baseline setup and action rules and must be applied literally. Commonly missed rules are timing windows for fast and slow powers, push and gather legality, and the difference between destroy and damage.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Spirit Island is cooperative and has no normal point total. Win immediately when the current terror-level victory condition is met. At Terror I all invaders must be removed, at Terror II all cities, at Terror III all towns and cities. Loss occurs if a loss condition triggers first, including blight collapse or fear deck exhaustion. Resolve timing strictly in phase order when win and loss effects are close.

beginner_strategy.content: Stop early ravages instead of chasing flashy kills. Coordinate spirit roles so one controls position while another delivers damage or fear. Use fast powers to break build and explore chains before they snowball. Place presence aggressively enough to improve range and card-play tracks. Draft major powers only when your reclaim and energy engine can support them.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint with rulebook-text as primary source.
